channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Yasmien Kurdi seeks DepEd’s help over daughter’s reported bullying incident
item.description: Actress Yasmien Kurdi has officially filed a complaint with the Department of Education (DepEd) after her daughter allegedly experienced bullying at school. The Kapuso star expressed deep concern for ...

item.title: US and Iran Conduct Third Round of Talks in Islamabad Mediated by Pakistan
item.description: In Islamabad, the United States and Iran have entered their third round of direct talks, mediated by Pakistan, aiming to find a breakthrough in resolving ongoing tensions. These discussions follow a t...

item.title: Donald Trump Attends UFC Event in Miami Amid Heightened Global Tensions
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump made a public appearance on Saturday night at a UFC event held in Miami, Florida. The visit came during a weekend marked by heightened international tension followin...

item.title: Oga Obinna Responds to Mbavu Destroyer Lawsuit, Suggests Rematch as Solution
item.description: Kenya's popular show host Oga Obinna has confirmed that he is being sued by boxer Mbavu Destroyer following the contentious Vurugu fight. Mbavu has raised several complaints including an alleged groin...

item.title: Makhudu Sefara argues Steenhuisen should remain in cabinet, not be made an ambassador
item.description: In his opinion piece for TimesLIVE, Makhudu Sefara reflects on the end of John Steenhuisen’s term as leader of South Africa’s Democratic Alliance (DA), following his emotional farewell at the party’s ...

item.title: Ceasefire Between Russia and Ukraine Breaks During Orthodox Easter
item.description: The ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine meant to cover Orthodox Easter was largely unsuccessful. Ukrainian authorities reported almost 470 violations, including air strikes, drone attacks, and shelli...

item.title: Trump’s criticism of Australia affects Republican support for the alliance
item.description: Recent polling conducted by YouGov for Joe Hockey's Bondi Partners has revealed that former US President Donald Trump’s repeated criticisms of Australia are causing a decline in previously strong Repu...

item.title: Animated Short ‘Jour de Vent’ Brings Everyday Moments to Life Through Motion and Emotion
item.description: The article introduces Jour de Vent (“Windy Day”), an award-winning animated short film created by graduates of École des Nouvelles Images. Set in an ordinary park, the film transforms a calm afternoo...

item.title: Strengthening Youth Engagement in South African Democracy Beyond Digital Platforms
item.description: South Africa is facing a steady decline in democratic engagement, particularly among young people, with voter turnout dropping to just 58.6% in 2024. The IEC has recently launched a TV series and podc...

item.title: A heartfelt look at Netflix’s ‘18th Rose’: Young love and provincial dreams intertwined
item.description: The Netflix film ‘18th Rose’ offers a sweet and emotional look at young love and the pursuit of dreams in a provincial setting. Directed with sensitivity and charm, the movie captures the coming-of-ag...

item.title: Bus crash in Canary Islands leaves one British tourist dead and another critically injured
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in the Canary Islands when a bus accident resulted in the death of a British tourist, while another remains in serious condition. Authorities reported that the collision inv...

item.title: Majembe Collects KSh 2 Million Car Prize and Talks About Plans with Family
item.description: Kenyan boxer Majembe has finally claimed the grand prize following his win against Mbavu Destroyer on April 4, 2026. On April 10, he was seen happily testing his new Toyota Noah, a KSh 2 million car s...

item.title: Wike’s faction reclaims PDP headquarters after court ruling; Turaki camp rejects move
item.description: The internal crisis inside the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) don still dey escalate as the camp wey dey loyal to Nyesom Wike, the Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, don take over the party he...

item.title: Hundreds detained at London protest over Palestine Action ban rule
item.description: More than 500 demonstrators were arrested in central London during the first major protest opposing the government’s proscription of Palestine Action since the High Court ruled the group’s ban unlawfu...

item.title: Responding to the 2026 Oil Crisis: Lessons and Community Initiatives
item.description: The 2026 oil crisis is hitting the Philippines hard, primarily through rising fuel prices, affecting transport, agriculture, and the overall cost of living. Jeepney drivers have reduced or stopped ope...

item.title: Digital innovations driving smarter and more efficient infrastructure in Kenya
item.description: Digital tools are changing the face of Kenya’s infrastructure development in a big way. With the adoption of technologies such as smart sensors, data analytics, artificial intelligence, and GIS mappin...

item.title: US-Iran Talks Continue Amidst Strait of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: High-level peace talks between the United States and Iran are ongoing, entering a second day with deep concerns surrounding the vital Strait of Hormuz. Vice President J.D. Vance’s recent meeting with ...

item.title: Building a resilient Nairobi through integrated urban planning
item.description: Nairobi, Kenya’s busy capital, is growing fast — but this rapid urbanisation has exposed weak infrastructure and poor governance. Recent floods that claimed lives show how the city’s systems are strug...

item.title: Canadian Rangers Complete 52-Day Snowmobile Patrol Across Northern Territories Ending in Churchill
item.description: Members of the 1st Canadian Ranger Patrol Group concluded a 52-day snowmobile patrol across Canada's North, finishing in Churchill, Manitoba. Beginning near the Yukon-Alaska border in February, the jo...

item.title: Urban Infrastructure in India is Neglected Despite Rising Budgets
item.description: In India, municipal infrastructure is increasingly neglected despite rising budgets and ambitious urban projects. Cities like Indore, celebrated as clean and modern, experience tragic incidents such a...
